summary: Iteration 31 - Live Predictor 4 Fixes testing complete. All 32 backend tests passed. All 4 fixes verified: (1) Non-linear alpha curve with piecewise decay 0.85→0.20→0.05, (2) Rebalanced H weights with H2H reduced to 0.10 and venue/form increased, (3) Squad strength differential at 0.22 weight, (4) Venue-specific par scores (wankhede:178, chinnaswamy:195, chepauk:158). Frontend displays Alpha/H/L summary boxes, H breakdown with 5 factors, L breakdown with 6 factors, and venue profile correctly.
passed_tests: Backend: compute_alpha returns 0.85 at 0 balls (pre-game)
Backend: compute_alpha returns ~0.525 at 60 balls (mid inn1)
Backend: compute_alpha returns ~0.20 at 120 balls (end inn1)
Backend: compute_alpha returns ~0.125 at 180 balls (mid inn2)
Backend: compute_alpha returns 0.05 at 240 balls (end match)
Backend: Alpha never goes below 0.05
Backend: H weights sum to 1.0 (0.22+0.10+0.28+0.25+0.15)
Backend: H2H weight is 0.10 (reduced from 0.40)
Backend: Venue weight is 0.28
Backend: Form weight is 0.25
Backend: compute_squad_strength_differential function exists
Backend: Squad strength returns 0.5 for empty data
Backend: Squad strength returns ~0.5 for equal teams
Backend: Squad strength weight is 0.22 in H calculation
Backend: VENUE_PAR_SCORES dictionary exists with 14 venues
Backend: Wankhede par is 178
Backend: Chinnaswamy par is 195
Backend: Chepauk par is 158
Backend: All venue profiles have par, bat_first_win_pct, dew_risk
Backend: get_venue_profile returns correct profile for known venues
Backend: get_venue_profile returns default (par:170) for unknown venues
Backend: compute_live_prediction returns alpha in response
Backend: compute_live_prediction returns H and L in response
Backend: compute_live_prediction returns H_breakdown with 5 factors
Backend: compute_live_prediction returns L_breakdown with 6 factors
Backend: compute_live_prediction returns venue_profile
Backend: API health check returns version 4.1.0
Backend: server.py imports compute_live_prediction
Backend: server.py passes xi_data (cached_xi) to compute_live_prediction
Backend: fetch-live endpoint exists
Backend: fetch-live returns weightedPrediction with alpha/H/L
Backend: match state endpoint works
Frontend: LiveMatch page loads with data-testid='live-match-page'
Frontend: Fetch Live Scores button works
Frontend: Weighted Prediction panel displays
Frontend: Alpha/H/L summary boxes display (Alpha: 0.731, H: 58.3%, L: 45.1%)
Frontend: H breakdown shows Squad Strength (0.22), Venue (0.28), Form (0.25), Toss (0.15), H2H (0.10)
Frontend: L breakdown shows Score vs Par (0.30), Wickets (0.25), Recent Rate (0.15), Bowlers (0.15), Pre-match (0.10), Context (0.05)
Frontend: Venue profile shows Par, Bat 1st %, Dew risk
Frontend: Dual Prediction Models section displays
Frontend: Claude Opus Prediction panel displays
Frontend: Model Consensus indicator shows HIGH CONSENSUS
Frontend: Formula info button opens modal with P(win) = alpha × H + (1-alpha) × L
critical_code_review_comments: live_predictor.py correctly implements non-linear alpha curve with piecewise decay
H weights correctly sum to 1.0: squad(0.22) + h2h(0.10) + venue(0.28) + form(0.25) + toss(0.15)
compute_squad_strength_differential uses sigmoid function for balanced output
VENUE_PAR_SCORES contains 14 venues with par, bat_first_win_pct, dew_risk
server.py line 456 passes cached_xi to compute_live_prediction
LiveMatch.js displays all H and L breakdown factors with correct weights
test_credentials: No authentication required - open access app
seed_data_creation: None - used existing live match ipl2026_071 (KKR vs PBKS)
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 31 features verified. The live predictor now uses alpha-blended H×L model with: (1) Non-linear alpha decay 0.85→0.20→0.05, (2) H weights: squad 0.22, h2h 0.10, venue 0.28, form 0.25, toss 0.15, (3) Squad strength from Playing XI at 0.22 weight, (4) Venue-specific par scores. Match ipl2026_071 (KKR vs PBKS at Eden Gardens) was used for live testing. The formula P(win) = alpha × H + (1-alpha) × L is displayed in the UI with full breakdown.
